title: The Comeback

jokes.text: Am I finally getting a baby brother?
jokes.setup: Jerry's parents want to talk to him about 'something'
jokes.punchline: Jerry assumes it's about having another child at their advanced age
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry character moment - sarcastic deflection of serious conversation with absurd misreading of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: Jerry, we want to talk to you about something.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're having another baby?

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, what else could it be? You're calling me in here like it's some big announcement.

jokes.text: Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Jerry learning his parents already sold the Cadillac he bought them
jokes.punchline: Jerry's stunned reaction to their casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat - economy of words conveys complete exasperation

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You sold it? You sold my car?

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: We didn't need it.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You didn't need it? I bought you a Cadillac!

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: It was too much car for us.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Too much car? It's a Cadillac, not a spaceship!

jokes.text: No, no, no, it's not the sex, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Kramer says Emily is wearing him out
jokes.punchline: Unprompted clarification about what's NOT wearing him out
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer overshare - perfectly timed preemptive clarification reveals his priorities and social obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Emily is wearing me out.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: But the sex? The sex is not wearing me out.

jokes.text: Yeah, that Def Jam is a force.
jokes.setup: Parents read article saying stand-up comedy isn't what it used to be
jokes.punchline: Morty's confident assessment of comedy trends he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Perfect parent character moment - authoritative tone about something he's completely out of touch with

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: You know, these comedians today, they don't have it. They're not like the comedians of my day.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: Morty, you never go to see comedians.

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: I don't have to go see them. I read about them. It's all here in the paper. Comedy isn't what it used to be.

jokes.text: Now, this Bloomingdale thing: That could be the next wave.
jokes.setup: Parents suggesting Jerry quit comedy
jokes.punchline: Helen treating retail management as cutting-edge career move
jokes.explanation: Absurd parental logic - treating mundane career path as revolutionary opportunity

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: You should quit the comedy. It's not a real job.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Your mother's right. You should get into retail management.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: Yes! That's a real career. My cousin's son is a assistant manager at the Gap. He's doing very well for himself.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: The Gap? Mom, I'm performing in front of hundreds of people.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: So? He's managing people. That's much more important.

jokes.text: It's PLO blend.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Arabian Mocha Java coffee being strong
jokes.punchline: George's politically incorrect wordplay about Palestinian Liberation Organization
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical wordplay with edge - risky humor that works through pure word association

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's a strong coffee. That's like the PLO of coffees.

jokes.text: She's sticking it that she makes more money... Not nice. Sticking it to me.
jokes.setup: Elaine buys George coffee
jokes.punchline: George's paranoid interpretation of a kind gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurosis - escalating paranoia over innocent act, with great repetitive phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I got you a coffee.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Why? What do you want from me? You never just buy me coffee. There's always an angle with you people.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I was just being nice!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Nice? Nobody's nice for no reason. You want something. You want to borrow money, you want to set me up with your friend... What is it?

jokes.text: Sticking it!
jokes.setup: Continued argument about coffee purchase
jokes.punchline: George's increasingly unhinged insistence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation callback - George's obsessive repetition becoming more manic

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, I paid for that coffee! I have a receipt!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going back to that coffee shop right now and I'm demanding a full investigation!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: This is it. This is the thing that breaks me. Not my job, not my relationships — a cup of coffee!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'm contacting the Better Business Bureau, the health department, and the FBI if I have to!

jokes.text: Aunt Baby.
jokes.setup: Discussion of family member who died at age 7
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name for someone who died so young
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist name - darkly funny that someone called 'Baby' never lived past childhood

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He died when he was seven.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Seven? That's very young. What was his name?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Mooby.

jokes.text: She's got the jimmy legs.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining why he doesn't want Emily in bed after sex
jokes.punchline: Kramer's invented term for restless leg syndrome
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer neologism - 'jimmy legs' became part of pop culture lexicon, perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I don't want her in the bed after sex. She's got the jimmy legs.

jokes.text: You might be more than just a couple of tweaks away from a healthy relationship.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking for relationship advice about kicking women out after sex
jokes.punchline: Elaine's massive understatement about his relationship issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement - clinical assessment of someone fundamentally broken

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Kramer, you can't just kick women out of your apartment after sex. You have to be nice to them. You have to care about them.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I do care about them.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: No, you don't. You kick them out!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want me to do?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I think you might have some issues with women, Kramer.

jokes.text: Well, you're not exactly zeroing in yourself, lady.
jokes.setup: Elaine critiquing Kramer's relationship skills
jokes.punchline: Kramer's oblivious counter-attack on her love life
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kramer's completely unaware deflection hits close to home

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Kramer, you have no idea how to treat a woman. Your relationships never work out.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from you. At least I'm not dating every guy in Manhattan and then wondering why they all turn out to be losers.

jokes.text: Carrot Top.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining last-minute gig at Bally's casino
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's replacing famously bad comedian
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference joke - single name reveals Jerry's career status

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I got a last-minute gig at Bally's. I'm replacing this guy who had to leave suddenly.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Apparently, the audience demanded a refund before he even finished his first joke.

jokes.text: They're yelling and yelling and then one day, they're all gone.
jokes.setup: Discussion of family members dying young from 'internal problems'
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact description of stress-related deaths
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny family analysis - Frank's clinical assessment of Costanza anger issues

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: My father, he died of internal problems. My mother, she died of internal problems. My brother, he died of internal problems.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: What kind of internal problems?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: They were all nervous people. They couldn't relax.

jokes.text: She'd never make it.
jokes.setup: George asking how old Aunt Baby would be if alive today
jokes.punchline: Frank's certain assessment she couldn't survive to present day
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark callback - Frank's matter-of-fact fatalism about family genetics

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: How old would Aunt Baby be if she were alive today?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Dead. She'd be dead.

jokes.text: Like strawberry pie.
jokes.setup: Emily asking if Kramer enjoys lovemaking
jokes.punchline: Kramer's oddly specific food comparison for sex
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Kramer analogy - unexpectedly wholesome comparison in sexual context

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Do you enjoy lovemaking?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It's like a pastrami sandwich.

jokes.text: You got drugs hidden in the trunk?
jokes.setup: Jerry wanting to buy back the Cadillac he gave his parents
jokes.punchline: Jack's suspicious assumption about Jerry's motives
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jack's paranoid Florida retiree mindset

dialogue.speaker: Jack Klompus
dialogue.line: You want to buy back the car? What are you, some kind of wise guy? You're gonna take it, sell it for parts!

jokes.text: You're not me.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out Jack bought the car for six thousand
jokes.punchline: Jack's simple but revealing explanation for price difference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - three words capture entire power dynamic and Jack's opportunism

dialogue.speaker: Jack Klompus
dialogue.line: Well, it's a different car.

jokes.text: Still works.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if Jack needs a pen to write the check
jokes.punchline: Jack's proud demonstration of his retrieved pen
jokes.explanation: Great callback to famous pen episode - Jack's petty satisfaction at recovering his property

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Do you need a pen to write the check?

dialogue.speaker: Jack Klompus
dialogue.line: A pen? I got a pen!

dialogue.speaker: Jack Klompus
dialogue.line: I found this pen. It was in the street. Look at this, it still writes!

jokes.text: I used to have a problem.
jokes.setup: Kramer somehow knowing Jerry's hotel room number
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of past gambling addiction
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal - Kramer's matter-of-fact admission explains random knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Oh, I know all the good hotels in the city. I used to have a gambling problem.

jokes.text: I was registered under 'Slappy White.'
jokes.setup: Jerry not being registered at Bally's under his own name
jokes.punchline: Using an obscure old comedian's name as alias
jokes.explanation: Great inside comedy reference - using forgotten performer's name shows Jerry's comedy nerd credentials

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So I'm at Bally's, right? And they tell me I'm not registered under my name. I said, 'What do you mean I'm not registered?' They said, 'You're registered under Carnac the Magnificent.' I said, 'Who is Carnac the Magnificent?' Apparently, I used some old comedian's name when I signed up. I don't even remember doing it!

jokes.text: We're having an upscale dinner.
jokes.setup: Unexpected appearance of George's parents at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Frank's proud announcement of their lifestyle upgrade
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Frank treating basic restaurant meal as luxury experience

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We're living like kings! I'm telling you, we moved out to the front of the building.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We got a whole new life ahead of us.

jokes.text: That didn't sound like the Golden Nugget.
jokes.setup: Jerry claiming call was from Golden Nugget casino
jokes.punchline: George's suspicious observation about the phone call
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for Jerry's deception - George's paranoid instincts accidentally right

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. You're telling me the Golden Nugget called you? Why would they call you?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe they got my number somehow.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: That doesn't make sense. The Golden Nugget doesn't just call people randomly. What did they want?

jokes.text: This dry air is curing me like a Black Forest ham.
jokes.setup: Morty commenting on office environment
jokes.punchline: Bizarre food preservation metaphor for feeling better
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor - comparing human comfort to meat curing process is perfectly bizarre

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: You know, I was thinking about something. When you're not feeling well, it's like you're a piece of fish, and you're sitting out on the counter. But then someone puts you in a jar of mayonnaise, and suddenly you feel better. You're still fish, but now you're preserved fish. You're good to go.

jokes.text: Who's Mr. Fancy?
jokes.setup: Peterman returning to find Morty in expensive suit
jokes.punchline: Peterman's dismissive question about the well-dressed stranger
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Peterman's condescending assessment of Morty's appearance

dialogue.speaker: Peterman
dialogue.line: Who is this?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: That's my father.

dialogue.speaker: Peterman
dialogue.line: Your father? He looks like a millionaire!

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: Well, I'm wearing thesuit.

jokes.text: There was a man. He was trying to get into my apartment last night. He was jiggling the doorknob for 25 minutes.
jokes.setup: Emily explaining why she doesn't want to sleep alone
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and persistent attempted break-in
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail - the 25-minute specificity makes the story more ridiculous than scary

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: I don't want to sleep alone. What if someone tries to break in?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So you want me to stay over to protect you?

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: Yes, exactly.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Emily, what are the odds someone's going to break into your apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: I don't know, but what if it's a very determined burglar who specifically targets women living alone in this neighborhood, and he has a very detailed map of my apartment that he got from a disgruntled landlord, and he's been casing the place for weeks, and he knows my exact sleep schedule, and he's waiting for a night when I'm alone because he has a very specific grudge against me personally?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's oddly detailed.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: I know, but it could happen.

jokes.text: No, it was a fearless cat burglar.
jokes.setup: Kramer suggesting it was just the wind
jokes.punchline: Emily's dramatic characterization of persistent doorknob jiggler
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - 'fearless cat burglar' is absurdly dramatic description for doorknob jiggling

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It was just the wind.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: The wind? The wind doesn't jiggle doorknobs, Kramer. Someone was trying to get in here.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, Jerry, I'm very lucky that those crocs didn't get me.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the car accident in the Everglades
jokes.punchline: Adding alligator danger to the car crash story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Jack adding dramatic wildlife encounter to already absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Jack Klompus
dialogue.line: So there I am in the Everglades, I'm backing up, I look around, I don't see nothing. I'm backing up some more, I hear a little sound. I look down, I've backed right into a swamp. The car's stuck. I'm in the car, it's sinking, and all of a sudden, snap! An alligator comes up and gets ahold of the car!

jokes.text: You know, that almost makes this all worthwhile.
jokes.setup: Jack losing Jerry's expensive pen in the swamp
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic silver lining about the disaster
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution - Jerry's satisfaction at pen's destruction after all the trouble it caused

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh, well, look on the bright side. At least the alligators are gonna have really nice handwriting.

jokes.text: I won't ride in a German car.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they didn't buy a Mercedes
jokes.punchline: Helen's principled stand based on WWII grudges
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Helen's long-held historical grudges affecting car purchases

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.line: We're not buying a Mercedes. My father fought in World War II.

jokes.text: I know a couple of Chinamen over on 43rd Street that will do it for half that.
jokes.setup: Discussion of expensive silk fabric sourcing
jokes.punchline: Morty's casually racist business suggestion in corporate meeting
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment - Morty's outdated terminology creating workplace awkwardness

jokes.text: Estelle has got the jimmy arms.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he and Estelle sleep in separate beds
jokes.punchline: Extension of Kramer's 'jimmy legs' to arm movement
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback expansion - takes Kramer's neologism and applies it to different body part

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We have separate beds. It's better for the circulation.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I got jimmy arms. I'm flailing around all night.

jokes.text: Like you wouldn't believe.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking if you can get jimmy legs in your arms
jokes.punchline: Frank's emphatic confirmation of the severity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Frank's dramatic emphasis on Estelle's sleep disruption

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Can you get Jimmy legs in your arms?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, you can get it in your arms, your legs, your face! It's a very serious condition!

jokes.text: And so I made an explosive out of chickpeas.
jokes.setup: Peterman telling adventure story to keep Morty at work late
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous improvised weapon from legumes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman absurdity - chickpea explosives perfectly captures his grandiose storytelling

dialogue.speaker: Peterman
dialogue.line: I was trapped in a cave in Peru with nothing but a sack of beans. I fashioned a crude weapon from the legumes, tied them together with vines. The jaguar didn't know what hit it.

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: A weapon made of beans?

dialogue.speaker: Peterman
dialogue.line: Beans, Morty. Don't underestimate the bean.

jokes.text: Cheap fabric and dim lighting: That's how you move merchandise.
jokes.setup: Morty criticizing Peterman's business model
jokes.punchline: Cynical but practical retail wisdom
jokes.explanation: Sharp business observation - Morty's practical retail experience vs Peterman's fantasies

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: You know, Peterman, you're running this business all wrong. You got too much overhead, too much waste. You gotta streamline, cut costs where it matters.

dialogue.speaker: Peterman
dialogue.line: But Morty, my customers expect a certain level of quality and service.

dialogue.speaker: Morty
dialogue.line: Quality? Service? That's what's killing you! People don't want quality, they want cheap. They want fast. Give 'em cheap and fast, they'll buy anything.

jokes.text: What are you, sticking it to me?
jokes.setup: Elaine mentioning George's missed stock options
jokes.punchline: George's paranoid catchphrase returning
jokes.explanation: Solid callback - George's paranoia returning at moment of actual financial loss

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Why didn't anybody tell me about this? This could've been huge for me!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: These pretzels are making me thirsty.

jokes.text: I saw a bum sleeping in the Cadillac the other day.
jokes.setup: Discussion of checking on the car obsessively
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact observation about car being used as shelter
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor - Frank's casual mention of serious situation

dialogue.speaker: Frank Costanza
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, if I don't check on that car every twenty minutes, I'm gonna have a nervous breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Frank Costanza
dialogue.line: A guy could live in that car. It's got a trunk, a back seat... somebody's probably living in it right now.

jokes.text: They don't nap. They make it their home. They urinate in there.
jokes.setup: Frank's comment about bum sleeping in car
jokes.punchline: Estelle's graphic escalation of the homeless situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Estelle's blunt progression from napping to property damage

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: A bum? A bum sleeping in our car? Frank, this is a catastrophe! Do you know what could happen? He could use our upholstery as a toilet! He could invite his bum friends over for a bum convention in our driveway! Next thing you know, there's bums everywhere - in the bushes, in the garage, eating our food! We'll have to move! We'll have to leave Queens! We'll end up living in a cardboard box ourselves, and then we'll BE the bums!

tmdb_overview: Kramer arranges to die with dignity; Jerry learns his tennis consultant is a lousy player; George becomes obsessed with responding to an insult long after the fact; Elaine offends her video advisor.
insight: George's comeback obsession generates 38 jokes at steady pace, missing elite tier by narrow margin.
